zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale於1240年出生在蘇格蘭洛包爾卡斯特萊頓時，他的父親Nicholas de Soules40歲，母親Annora Normanville28歲。他和Ermengarde Durward至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1321年4月20日在蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾度巴頓享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale于1240年出生在苏格兰洛包爾卡斯特萊頓时，他的父亲Nicholas de Soules40岁，母亲Annora Normanville28岁。他和Ermengarde Durward至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1321年4月20日在苏格兰登巴頓史爾度巴頓享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Soulis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Greek: possibly a nickname derived from Albanian shul ‘bolt of wood’, figuratively ‘brawny man’ and ‘tall dolt’.
Americanized form of Lithuanian Šaulys (see Shaulis ).
Scottish (Ayrshire): variant of   Soule  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Shaulis
meanings: Altered form of Lithuanian Šaulys: occupational name from šaulys ‘rifleman’. Compare Soulis .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale was born in 1240, in Castleton, Roxburghshire, Scotland, his father, Nicholas de Soules, was 40 and his mother, Annora Normanville, was 28. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Ermengarde Durward. He died on 20 April 1321, in Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale est né en 1240, à Castleton, Roxburghshire, Écosse, son père, Nicholas de Soules, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Annora Normanville, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Ermengarde Durward. Il est décédé le 20 avril 1321, à Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale 1240 in Castleton, Roxburghshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Nicholas de Soules 40 und seine Mutter Annora Normanville 28. Er hatte mit Ermengarde Durward mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 20. April 1321 im Alter von 81 Jahren in Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale è nato il nel 1240 nel Castleton, Roxburghshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Nicholas de Soules, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Annora Normanville, aveva 28 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Ermengarde Durward. Morì il 20 aprile 1321, nel Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, all'età di 81 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdaleが1240年にスコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアカストレトンで生まれた時，父Nicholas de Soulesは40歳，母Annora Normanvilleは28歳であった。彼はErmengarde Durwardとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1321年4月20日にスコットランドダンバートンシュアイアダンバートンで81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale(이)가 1240년 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르 카스틀레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas de Soules(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Annora Normanville(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Ermengarde Durward(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1321년 4월 20일 스컷랜드 던바토실르 드바턴에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale nasceu em 1240, em Castleton, Roxburghshire, Scotland, seu pai, Nicholas de Soules, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Annora Normanville, tinha 28 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Ermengarde Durward. Ele faleceu em 20 de abril de 1321, em Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, com 81 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale родился в 1240 г. в местности кастлетон, роксбургшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Nicholas de Soules было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Annora Normanville, 28. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Ermengarde Durward. Он умер 20 апреля 1321 г. в местности думбартон, дунбартоншир, Шотландия в возрасте 81 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William De Soulis Lord Of Liddesdale nació en 1240, en Castleton, Roxburghshire, Scotland, su papá, Nicholas de Soules, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Annora Normanville, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con Ermengarde Durward. Murió el 20 de abril de 1321, en Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, a la edad de 81 años.
